mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.03 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline when either series declines, or combined positive signals yield negative forward returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining inflation readings during a period of elevated policy rates signal an imminent monetary policy pivot, compressing term premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of instances when CPIAUCSL falls during high-rate environments, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and economic momentum, which supports corporate earnings and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth correlates with negative forward returns in >58% of observations, or the rolling information coefficient remains below 0.01 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 150,000 signal robust labor market expansion, boosting household income and consumer spending forecasts, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns in >60% of triggered months, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated federal funds rates and a rising unemployment rate indicates monetary policy is overheating the labor market, increasing recession probability and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative returns, with positive outcomes occurring in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid real GDP growth coupled with persistent inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, raising the probability of a hard landing and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of periods when GDP growth exceeds 3% alongside high inflation, or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below 0.2 in out-of-sample testing.
